cot_prefix: Okay, let's figure out the answer to this question. The question is: Why has smog become a common concern? The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to look at the relevant part of the text provided. The question is asking about the reason smog is a common concern. Let me find the part of the passage that talks about this. The passage says: "Smog is especially a common concern. As a popular online post said, air may be the only thing that is equal for everyone, despite your income or profession. People with higher incomes are able to drink only bottled spring water and eat only organic food by paying higher prices, but they breathe the same air as everyone else." This part explains that smog is a common concern because everyone, regardless of their income or profession, has to breathe the same air. The key point here is that you can't avoid the air you breathe, unlike water or food where you might pay more to get cleaner options. Looking at the options: A) Because people have to pay higher prices. The text mentions higher prices for water and food, but smog is about air, which you can't pay to avoid. So A is not correct. B) Because a popular online post discussed it. The post is mentioned as part of the reason, but the main point is the equality aspect of air, not just the post itself. It's a supporting detail, not the main reason. C) Because we have to develop industry. The passage does talk about economic development, but the question is why smog is a concern. The fact that we have to develop industry is more about the cause of smog, not why it's a common concern. D) Because nobody can
